//: Mirador backend dev container — zero-install onboarding + Codespaces-ready.
Provides every tool a fresh contributor needs to build + run tests + spin
the full compose stack locally, via a single `code --folder-uri` or the
'Open in Codespaces' button on the GitHub mirror. Rationale:
- The host laptop keeps zero Java / Maven / Node / Docker-CLI install
state — everything lives in the container, so version drift between
- On Codespaces, the first boot is one click; after that the container
caches (~/.m2, node_modules via volume mount) persist across reboots.
- Locally via VS Code + Dev Containers extension: same image, same
tooling. `git commit` / `glab mr create` all work inside.
Image choice — microsoft/java:25 base gives us JDK 25 + Maven 3.9.x
adds node/npm for when the UI is mounted side-by-side (see the UI's
own devcontainer.json for the UI-first flow).
Not yet covered — SSH commit signing. Contributors sign locally today;
flipping required_signatures on the GitHub mirror (TASKS.md: 'S2
signed-commits follow-up') will require adding an allowed_signers file
name: Mirador backend (Java 25 + Maven + Docker)
//features: Add tools this image doesn't ship with. Each feature is installed in a dedicated layer for cache-friendly rebuilds.
//customizations: VS Code-side tuning. The extensions below match the ones the team uses on the host; skipping to zero-config.
//postCreate: Runs once per container creation. Warms Maven + npm caches so the first mvn verify doesn't pay the full dep download cost.
//forwardPorts: Ports exposed by docker-compose that a contributor will want to hit from the host browser.
//remoteUser: Non-root per devcontainers best practice; matches the Dockerfile runtime user (UID 1000).
//mounts: Persist Maven + npm caches across container rebuilds — saves ~2 min on each re-open.
